figures_detected.description: Piston ring gap positioning diagram showing oil ring expander, top ring, oil ring upper rail, oil ring lower rail, and 2nd ring positions relative to front of engine
Main bearing layout diagram showing upper bearing, thrust bearing (only upper), No.5 bearing, oil hole, lower bearing identification color, oil groove, No.1 bearing, and front direction
Main bearing beam bolt tightening sequence diagram showing numerical order of bolt tightening
Illustration showing angle wrench being used to tighten main bearing beam bolts 60°-65° clockwise

steps.text: Set main bearings in their proper positions on cylinder block and main bearing cap.
notes: Confirm that correct main bearings are used. Refer to "Inspection".

steps.text:
Install crankshaft and main bearing beam and tighten bolts as follows:
[symbol]: Main bearing beam bolts
(1) Tighten to 25 to 30 N·m (2.5 to 3.1 kg-m, 18 - 22 ft-lb) in order shown.
(2) Turn bolts to 60° to 65° degrees clockwise with an angle wrench in order shown.

content_blocks.text: Prior to tightening bearing cap bolts, place bearing cap in its proper position by shifting crankshaft in the axial direction.
After securing bearing cap bolts, make sure crankshaft turns smoothly by hand.

tools_referenced.name: Angle wrench
tools_referenced.usage: Turn main bearing beam bolts 60° to 65° clockwise in order shown
